message:
perf(sync,bench): O(1) barrier waker slot tracking, re-import OwnedAcquireFuture, and fat LTO for benchmarks

BarrierWaitFuture slot-indexed waker management (src/sync/barrier.rs):
- Add `slot: usize` to WaitState::Waiting, recording the index where the
  waker was pushed into the waiters Vec
- On re-poll, use O(1) indexed lookup (slot < len && will_wake) before
  falling back to O(N) linear scan, avoiding quadratic behavior when many
  tasks wait on the same barrier
- Cancellation path uses O(1) swap_remove by slot index when valid,
  falling back to retain for robustness when the slot was invalidated
  by another waiter's cancellation
- Update Drop impl to use `..` pattern for the new slot field

ConcurrencyLimit import fix (src/service/concurrency_limit.rs):
- Re-import OwnedAcquireFuture for direct constructor usage after
  upstream refactor made it the preferred API entry point

Benchmark profile (Cargo.toml):
- Switch bench profile LTO from "thin" to "fat" for more aggressive
  cross-crate inlining in benchmark builds, producing more representative
  performance numbers at the cost of longer bench compile times

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
